subject: Public Health
title: John Snow and the Broad Street Pump
description: Learn John Snow and the Broad Street Pump in one minute.

hook.text: In 1854 London, cholera was killing hundreds. John Snow suspected the water, not bad air.

buildup.text: He mapped every death and noticed they clustered around one water pump on Broad Street.

discovery.text: Snow convinced officials to remove the pump handle. The outbreak slowed immediately.

twist.text: Germ theory didn't exist yet—Snow proved contaminated water was deadly before anyone knew why.

climax.text: His map became the founding act of modern epidemiology and public health investigation.

punchline.text: One map, one handle, one revolution in health.

quiz.question: What did John Snow's cholera investigation help found?
